sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md A3 (lines 118-129), Step-2 correction: 'da-windows (#49 ... merged) pins the strong form K >= W + C as S6 — the rule restates per §6e and conflict C3 is resolved by that ruling' (line 121)
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6c (candidate) + §6e S1-S6 da-windows (provisional pending DK) — decidable predicate over witnessed evidence
docs/core/DECISIONS.md entry 49 da-windows (PROVISIONAL pending DK ratification)

inputs.attackFlagText: DA agreement §6c describes the challenge outcome as having 'an objective, eventually-consistent answer' — 'eventually-consistent' is not a pure predicate; the B2 rule must be restated as decidable at a fixed height over a witnessed evidence set or the kernel cannot be pure.
inputs.predicateSignature: daChallengeOutcome(witnessedEvidenceSet, currentHeight, eligibleAt{H>=h+K}, W[#49 provisional=2], C[#49 provisional=3], K[#49 provisional=6], priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseBelowEligibleBoundary: the same anchor evaluated at currentHeight = h+K-1 (provisional K=6) -> not yet eligible; as a fixed-height predicate the not-yet-eligible claim confers no award; at h+K exactly it becomes eligible (pins the H>=h+K boundary block close-1/close)
fixture.caseS6Violation: a window parameterization where K < W + C violates the S6 strong-form invariant -> the kernel cannot produce a pure, replay-stable verdict; the windows must satisfy K >= W + C
fixture.WCK: #49 provisional consensus parameters (S7 provisional W=2, C=3, K=6); S2 eligibleAt(anchor,H,K):=H>=h+K; S6 strong form K >= W + C
fixture.priorState: baseline; a single witnessed evidence set, no qualifying bond

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): the A3 rule SHAPE (the §6c challenge outcome restated as a pure predicate decidable at a fixed height over the witnessed evidence set — S2 eligibleAt H>=h+K, S3 includable/holdsPriority, S4 evidence-in/verdict-out, identical on replay) is derivable, but the h+K eligibility boundary and the K >= W + C strong-form invariant are governed by #49 (PROVISIONAL — S2/S5/S6/S7). A3 is the precise concern #49 resolves; per the gate, depends-on-#49 ⇒ provisional-vector, never vector-now. Authored with provisional W=2, C=3, K=6; the verdict re-derives per flipMarker if DK rules #49 differently.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; flips/re-derives on DK's #49 ruling.

expected.invariant: the DA challenge outcome is a pure predicate decidable at a fixed height over the witnessed evidence set (S2 eligibleAt H>=h+K, S3 includable/holdsPriority, S4 evidence-in/verdict-out): below h+K the claim is not yet eligible and confers no award, and the windows must satisfy the strong form K >= W + C; identical on every deterministic replay
expected.rationale: This flag is the precise concern da-windows (#49) resolves: §6c's 'eventually-consistent' phrasing is restated as a decidable predicate over witnessed evidence at a fixed height. A3's verdict (decidable from witnessed inputs alone, identical on every replay, K >= W + C) is derivable, but it rides on #49 which is PROVISIONAL pending DK (S2 eligibleAt, S6 strong form, S7 provisional W=2/C=3/K=6). Per the gate, depends-on-#49 ⇒ provisional-vector, never vector-now.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK flips #49 to the weak W<=K form, re-pin the K >= W + C invariant and the include-then-retract foreclosure boundary; the W=K, C>0 include-then-retract case becomes admissible and the boundary verdict at h+K re-derives, while the decidable-at-fixed-height shape (S2/S3/S4 evidence-in/verdict-out) is stable, so the vector stays testable under the new window algebra.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md A11 (lines 210-218), flag (line 218); crossArea 'first-anchor-wins (earliest valid anchor) ... forfeited/excluded anchors confer no priority' (line 74)
docs/core/DECISIONS.md entry 26 (ratified, insertion-only) + entry 49 da-windows (provisional — includable/holdsPriority post-DA state)

inputs.attackFlagText: Uniqueness is enforced against kernel state, which depends on the DA verdicts of earlier batches: a name 'already inserted' by a later-forfeited batch must not block honest re-claiming — the insertion-uniqueness check must be defined over the post-DA-verdict state, and the spec does not yet say so.
inputs.predicateSignature: anchorAcceptance(candidateAnchor, priorKernelState{post-DA-verdict name state}, witnessedDAVerdicts{includable/holdsPriority per #49}, W[#49=2], C[#49=3], K[#49=6]) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseForfeitedThenReclaim: name N is inserted by batch B1 whose anchor later FORFEITS DA (excluded/never reaches eligibility at its window) -> B1 confers no priority; an honest re-claim of N at a fresh valid anchor B2 is ACCEPTED, because uniqueness is evaluated over the post-DA-verdict state in which B1's insertion was never finalized
fixture.caseIdempotentSameOwner: a same-(name,owner) re-claim at a fresh valid anchor is idempotent (accept, no double-insert)
fixture.postDaVerdictState: the kernel state AFTER each prior batch's DA verdict is applied (S3 includable/holdsPriority, #49 provisional); first-anchor-wins keys to the earliest VALID anchor (crossArea line 74)
fixture.priorState: name N marked inserted by B1 in pre-verdict state; B1 subsequently forfeits DA

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): A11 (anchors are insertion-only; a claim must not take an already-FINAL name) is cited on ratified #26 + candidate DA §5, but the flag's demand — insertion-uniqueness must be defined over the POST-DA-verdict state so a later-forfeited batch does not block honest re-claiming — consumes the eligibility verdict pinned by #49 S3 (includable/holdsPriority). The 'forfeited batch confers no priority / re-claim allowed' verdict rides on #49's eligibility algebra (and on first-anchor-wins = earliest-VALID-anchor, the subject of advisory spec-PR PR-5). Depends-on-#49 ⇒ provisional-vector, never vector-now. Authored with provisional W=2/C=3/K=6; re-derives per flipMarker.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; re-derives on DK's #49 ruling.

expected.invariant: insertion-uniqueness is evaluated over the post-DA-verdict kernel state: a name inserted by a batch whose anchor later forfeits DA confers no priority and does not block an honest re-claim at a fresh valid anchor; first-anchor-wins keys to the earliest VALID anchor; a same-(name,owner) re-claim is idempotent; identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: A11 (anchors are insertion-only; a claim must not take an already-final name) is cited on ratified DECISIONS #26 and candidate DA §5. The flag's specific demand — insertion-uniqueness must be defined over POST-DA-verdict state so a later-forfeited batch does not block honest re-claiming — is derivable, but the post-DA-verdict state IS the eligibility verdict pinned by da-windows (#49) S3 (includable/holdsPriority). The forfeited-batch-confers-no-priority / re-claim-allowed verdict depends on the #49 eligibility algebra and the first-anchor-wins (earliest-valid) merge. Depends on #49 ⇒ provisional-vector.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK flips #49's includable/holdsPriority semantics, re-pin the height at which a forfeited insertion stops blocking — what counts as 'post-DA-verdict state' moves with the C/W algebra; the insertion-only + earliest-valid-anchor shape (ratified #26) is stable, so the re-claim-accepted vector stays testable under the new eligibility algebra.

sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md A13 (lines 231-240), proposed test '(+) ... at close-1 it is still provisional (pins the >= comparison ... close-1, close, close+1)' + flag (line 240)
docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6e S2 da-windows (provisional) — 'Inclusive deadlines ... a witness at the deadline height exactly is in-window'

inputs.attackFlagText: Boundary off-by-one: legacy uses height >= closeHeight for finality (indexer.ts:517) but blockHeight <= closeHeight for in-window collision (indexer.ts:828) — events landing exactly at the close height are simultaneously 'in-window' and 'window-closed'; the hardening pass must pin one half-open interval or two implementations disagree on boundary-block bonds and collisions.
inputs.predicateSignature: noticeWindowOutcome(claim, currentHeight, anchorHeight, W_notice[launch-freeze], inclusiveDeadline[#49 S2 provisional], qualifyingBond?, priorKernelState) -> {verdict}

fixture.caseBondAfterClose: a qualifying bond submitted at currentHeight = close+1 (= anchorHeight + W_notice + 1) -> REJECTED as out-of-window; the window is closed and finality was already derived at close, so the late bond does not escalate
fixture.caseBoundaryTriple: the close-1 / close / close+1 triple pins one half-open interval: at close-1 the name is still provisional, at close (= anchorHeight + W_notice) it finalizes with no qualifying bond, and a bond exactly at close is in-window (escalates) per #49 S2 inclusive deadlines
fixture.windowForm: close = anchorHeight + W_notice; finality at currentHeight >= close (#37 >=); in-window inclusive through close (#49 S2 provisional); W_notice value is launch-freeze (parameterized)
fixture.priorState: a single in-window claim, no qualifying bond, no collision

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): A13 (notice window opens at the claim's earliest-valid-anchor height; outcome derived only at currentHeight >= anchorHeight + W_notice) is cited on ratified #37 bond-opens, and the off-by-one half-open-interval verdict is derivable — the >= comparator is ratified and #49 S2 pins inclusive deadlines consistently. But the close-height interval edge (in-window vs window-closed at exactly close) is fixed by #49 S2's inclusive-deadline algebra (PROVISIONAL). W_notice's value is launch-freeze (parameterized, not the blocker). Authored with #49 S2 inclusive; re-derives per flipMarker if DK rules S2 exclusive. NOT vector-now.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; re-derives on DK's #49 ruling.

expected.invariant: the notice window is pinned to ONE half-open interval keyed to the claim's earliest-valid-anchor height: in-window collision/bond is inclusive through close = anchorHeight + W_notice (#49 S2 inclusive deadline), finality is derived at currentHeight >= close (#37), and a qualifying bond at close+1 is out-of-window and does not escalate; the close-1/close/close+1 triple is identical on deterministic replay (W_notice value is launch-freeze; the interval form is #49 S2)
expected.rationale: A13 (notice window opens at the claim's earliest-valid-anchor height; outcome derived only at currentHeight >= anchorHeight + W_notice) is cited on ratified DECISIONS #37 bond-opens. The off-by-one half-open-interval verdict is derivable — the >= comparator is ratified and da-windows S2 pins inclusive deadlines (height <= h+X) consistently. But the boundary-block bond/collision pinning depends on the da-windows (#49) inclusive-deadline algebra (S2) to fix the close-height interval coherently; #49 is provisional. (W_notice value itself is launch-freeze, parameterized — not the blocker.)

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK flips #49's S2 inclusive deadline to exclusive, the half-open interval edge moves — a witness or bond AT close flips from in-window to out-of-window and the close-height bond/collision verdict re-derives; the >=-comparator finality (ratified #37) and the single-half-open-interval requirement are stable, so the close-1/close/close+1 triple stays testable under the new boundary convention.
